results.title: Puravankara records 28% growth in Q1 pre-sales, collections up 40%
results.description: Realty developer Puravankara reported a 28% year-on-year rise in pre-sales to ₹1,439 crore in the April-June quarter, while collections grew 40% to ₹1,199 crore. Average price realisation increased 18% to ₹10,589 per sq ft, and the company delivered 745 homes during the quarter.

results.title: SCERT Textbook error row: Odisha Crime Branch to begin probe, SP-rank officer to lead investigation
results.description: The Odisha Crime Branch is set to launch a detailed investigation into the serious errors detected in school textbooks. Earlier, Odisha CM Mohan Charan Majhi termed it a possible conspiracy and stressed the need for a high-level probe. Four-Member Team to Probe Root Cause of Serious Mistakes in School Textbooks According to sources, the investigation will be led by an officer of the rank of Superintendent of Police (SP). A dedicated four-member Crime Branch team has been constituted to examine every aspect of the case. Investigators will not only verify how the errors crept into the textbooks but will also trace the root cause and identify those responsible for the lapses. The move comes amid growing concern over glaring mistakes found in textbooks meant for school students. The Odisha government has taken the matter seriously, with the Chief Minister directing the Crime Branch to conduct an impartial and comprehensive investigation into the alleged irregularities. Also Read: Odisha CM Mohan Majhi orders Crime Branch probe into SCERT textbook errors Meanwhile, SCERT Director Bijay Kumar Sahu has proceeded to the Crime Branch office with all relevant documents related to the preparation, printing and publication of the textbooks. Sources said the SCERT Director is likely to lodge a formal FIR with the Crime Branch, paving the way for a criminal investigation into the matter. Officials said the probe will examine every stage of the textbook publication process, including content preparation, editing, proofreading, printing and approval, to determine whether the errors resulted from negligence or a deliberate act. The findings of the Crime Branch investigation are expected to play a crucial role in fixing accountability and ensuring that such lapses do not recur in the future, particularly in educational material meant for schoolchildren.

results.title: Gary Neville wades into Thomas Tuchel and Jude Bellingham row after England beat Norway
results.description: Gary Neville has backed Jude Bellingham after the England star appeared to reject Thomas Tuchel's critical assessment of the Three Lions' World Cup victory over Norway. The Three Lions secured a place in the semi-finals with a tense 2-1 extra-time triumph, recovering from a goal down as Bellingham struck twice to keep the nation's hopes of glory alive. Tuchel was delighted with the outcome but far less impressed by the performance, branding England "sloppy" and admitting they had been fortunate to progress. "Not happy with the performance," the England manager told ITV. TRENDING Stories Videos Your Say "We made life very, very difficult for ourselves today. "The result is fantastic, we're in the last four, it's amazing, but we're not happy with the performance. "The commitment is there but we made life very difficult for us in the way we played, how we played. "Sloppy, lot of technical mistakes, not fast enough, not repetitive enough. We were lucky today." Bellingham was informed of Tuchel's comments during his own television interview and initially responded: "Whatever." The Real Madrid midfielder later offered a more detailed defence of his team-mates, pointing to the difficult conditions and Norway's attacking quality. "Maybe he doesn't know what it takes to play in those conditions against Erling Haaland, Nusa, Sorloth," Bellingham said. "That's not an easy team to play against. LATEST SPORTS NEWS: Conor McGregor admits mental state in 'dark place' after UFC injury: 'I am in hell' Lionel Messi sends message to England ahead of World Cup semi-final Jannik Sinner set to lose huge amount in tax after winning Wimbledon title "I think we have tried to create a positive environment. We should continue that into the final four. "I can't speak highly enough of the lads. You're not going to win every game popping a thousand passes. Sometimes you have to win dirty." Neville has now praised Bellingham for fronting up after another demanding contest and refusing to hide from questions surrounding England's display. The former Manchester United defender believes the 23-year-old has embraced the responsibility that comes with being one of the team's biggest stars. "I really liked it," Neville said. "I'm not a great believer in players not doing interviews, particularly when you're the best players in the team. "If you're the star player, the guy that is going to win us games, I think you're the guy who has to go and speak." Neville also highlighted Bellingham's willingness to address the media throughout the tournament, including after less convincing results. "What I liked wasn't just the words that came out of his mouth, but it was the look in his eyes," he continued. "You see the look of someone who is fierce and determined to succeed. "He's 23 years of age. It's absolutely monumental what that lad is doing. "The impact that he is having on young people in our country and around the world is magnificent." Neville expects the contrasting post-match verdicts to attract attention but does not believe they represent a damaging problem. He argued that Tuchel's perfectionism and Bellingham's desire to defend the squad can coexist as England prepare to face Argentina in the last four on Wednesday. "Bellingham probably respects his manager for having the guts to say it after the game," Neville added. "He may not think it now, but in 10 or 15 years, when he thinks about excellence and elite performance, he will recognise Thomas Tuchel was only striving for more. "Thomas Tuchel knows that team can do better and knows they have to do better against Argentina. "He has a player who wants a bit of respect for getting to a World Cup semi-final and for the effort and commitment the players have put in. "I have no problem with this kind of stuff." Our Standards: The GB News Editorial Charter

results.title: WhatsApp, Telegram respond to MeitY notices over username feature
results.description: The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology’s (MeitY) on Thursday received a response from WhatsApp regarding the platform’s upcoming username feature, an official said without divulging details of the response. The deadline for submitting the response had ended on Thursday. The ministry had granted WhatsApp a four-day extension after the company sought additional time following a meeting with officials on July 2.
